Modes: --quick (core deps only), --fix (remediation suggestions)
JSON output conforms to envelope schema.

+{"id":"bd-11r","title":"Align XDG state/log layout with spec (daily logs + latest)","description":"# Align XDG State/Log Layout with Spec (daily logs + latest)

## Purpose
Ensure dsr writes logs, manifests, and artifacts in the exact XDG layout described in AGENTS.md. This standardizes status/report, notifications, prune, and debugging.

## Required Layout (authoritative)
```
~/.local/state/dsr/
├── logs/
│   ├── YYYY-MM-DD/
│   │   ├── run.log
│   │   └── builds/
│   │       └── *.log
│   └── latest -> YYYY-MM-DD
├── artifacts/
└── manifests/
```

## Implementation Notes
- Update logging to create date-based log dirs and set `run.log` as primary file
- Write per-host/per-target logs under `logs/YYYY-MM-DD/builds/`
- Update `logs/latest` symlink atomically (no partial state)
- Ensure build/release/status read the same layout
- No deletions outside dsr dirs (guardrails)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Logs land under date-based directories with `run.log`
- [ ] Per-build logs stored under `logs/YYYY-MM-DD/builds/`
- [ ] `logs/latest` always points to current date
- [ ] Artifacts + manifests stored under XDG paths
- [ ] status/report locates latest run without network calls","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:05.447817375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:38.986467900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11r","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:34.183013246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-11r","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:29.703658249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-11r","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:25.941201892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-11r","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:38.986432904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt","title":"EPIC: Fallback Release Infrastructure (ACFS)","description":"# Agent-Controlled Fallback System (ACFS) for Multi-Tool Releases

## Background & Problem Statement

GitHub Actions is limiting usage due to high volume (~$5k/month equivalent). When CI/CD jobs stay queued for >10 minutes, it indicates throttling. We need an automated fallback system that:

1. **Detects throttling** - Monitors GH Actions queue time, triggers fallback when jobs sit >10 min
2. **Builds locally** - Linux on Threadripper workstation (trj), macOS on Mac mini (mmini)
3. **Handles Windows** - Cross-compilation or other strategies TBD
4. **Uploads releases** - Manually create releases and upload binaries when throttled
5. **Smart installers** - curl|bash one-liners that try release first, build from source if stale

## Infrastructure Available

| Host | Alias | Purpose | Connection |
|------|-------|---------|------------|
| Threadripper Workstation | trj | Linux x86_64/ARM64 builds | local |
| Mac mini | mmini | macOS ARM64/x86_64 builds | ssh mmini |
| OVH servers | yto, fmd | Backup build capacity | ssh yto/fmd |

## Tools Affected (16 total from Dicklesworthstone toolchain)

cass, bv, cm, ubs, xf, ru, slb, caam, ntm, br, dcg, ms, wa, pt, rch, mcp_agent_mail

## Languages & Toolchains

- **Rust** (majority): cargo, rustup, cross-compilation via cargo-zigbuild or cross
- **Go**: go build, goreleaser  
- **TypeScript/Bun**: bun build, bun compile

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Any tool can be released within 30 minutes even when GH Actions is throttled
2. curl-bash installers work on Linux/macOS/Windows without manual intervention
3. Installers auto-detect stale releases (>10 commits behind) and offer source builds
4. Toolchain installation is automatic and non-destructive
5. All paths have unit tests and E2E validation scripts with detailed logging

## Key Design Decisions

1. **10-minute threshold**: Chosen as balance between patience and productivity
2. **Try release first**: Network download is faster than compilation for most users
3. **Commit-based staleness**: More accurate than date-based (active development = fresh releases)
4. **Non-destructive toolchains**: Never break existing installations  
5. **Detailed logging**: Every step logged for debugging failed installs

## Architecture Overview

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ACFS Release Pipeline                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [1] Push tag v1.2.3                                                │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] GH Actions triggered                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Queued <10min? ──→ Build & Release (normal path)       │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Queued >10min? ──→ ACFS Fallback Triggered             │
│                    │                                                 │
│                    ▼                                                 │
│              [3] Local Builds                                       │
│                    │                                                 │
│         ┌─────────┴─────────┬────────────────┐                      │
│         ▼                   ▼                ▼                      │
│    Linux (trj)        macOS (mmini)    Windows (cross)              │
│         │                   │                │                      │
│         └─────────┬─────────┴────────────────┘                      │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [4] Upload binaries to GitHub Release                  │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [5] Update Homebrew/Scoop formulas                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Smart Curl-Bash Installer Flow                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  curl -sSL install.sh | bash                                        │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [1] Detect OS/Arch                                                 │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] Check latest release                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Release exists & fresh (<10 commits behind)?           │
│         │         │                                                  │
│         │         └──→ Download binary ──→ Install ──→ Done         │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Release missing or stale?                              │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [3] Prompt: "Build from source?"                            │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [4] Check/Install toolchain (Rust/Go/Bun)                   │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [5] Clone repo & build                                      │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [6] Install binary ──→ Done                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Dependency Graph (high-level themes)

1. **Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring** - No deps, can start immediately
2. **Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure** - Needs monitoring to know when to trigger
3. **Theme 3: Release Upload Automation** - Needs builds to have artifacts to upload
4. **Theme 4: Smart Installers** - Can be developed in parallel, integrates with releases
5. **Theme 5: Testing & Validation** - Depends on all above being implemented

## Success Criteria
- [ ] End-to-end fallback pipeline works for all tools
- [ ] Installers detect stale releases and offer source builds
- [ ] Cross-platform builds + releases verified
- [ ] Unit/integration/E2E tests pass with detailed logs

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1","title":"Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection","description":"# Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection

## Purpose
Detect when GH Actions runs are throttled (queued >10 minutes) and automatically trigger the fallback build system.

## Rationale
- GH Actions throttling manifests as prolonged queue time
- We need **deterministic** detection using structured API data (no human output parsing)
- 10-minute threshold balances patience with productivity

## Implementation: Use GitHub REST API JSON
Use the workflow runs endpoint and compute queue time from timestamps:
```bash
json=$(gh_api "repos/$owner/$repo/actions/runs?per_page=20")
queued=$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.workflow_runs[] | select(.status=="queued") | .created_at')
```

## Key Components
1. **dsr check** - Check if GH Actions is throttled for a repo
2. **dsr watch** - Continuously monitor and trigger fallback when needed
3. **dsr status** - Show current queue status across all repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can detect a queued run within 1 minute of hitting 10min mark
- [ ] False positive rate <1%
- [ ] Works across all 16 tools' repositories
- [ ] Clear logging of detection and trigger events
- [ ] Exit codes: 0=OK, 1=throttled, 3=dependency/auth, 8=network

## Dependencies
- gh CLI (authenticated) OR GITHUB_TOKEN
- jq for JSON parsing
- date command with GNU extensions (or macOS-compatible fallback)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:08.469458787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.1","title":"Implement dsr check command - detect throttled runs","description":"# Implement `dsr check` — Detect Throttled GH Actions Runs

## Purpose
Detect when GitHub Actions is throttled (runs stuck in queue) and provide structured data for automation.

## Data Source (GitHub REST API)
Use workflow runs JSON from `repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` (via `gh api` or curl fallback). Avoid parsing human output.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr check <repo>
dsr check --all
 dsr check <repo> --threshold 10
 dsr check <repo> --json
```

## Detection Logic (robust)
1. Resolve short repo → full `owner/repo`
2. Fetch latest workflow runs via `gh_api` wrapper (with caching)
3. **Queued throttling**:
   - `status == "queued"` and `now - created_at > threshold`
4. **Stuck in_progress**:
   - `status == "in_progress"` and (`run_started_at == null` OR `now - run_started_at > threshold`)
5. Include `event`, `workflow_id`, `head_sha`, `html_url` for context

## Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "repo": "Dicklesworthstone/ntm",
  "status": "throttled",
  "oldest_queued_min": 15,
  "threshold_min": 10,
  "runs": [
    {"id":12345,"status":"queued","created_at":"...","workflow_id":999}
  ]
}
```

## Error Handling
- Auth missing → exit 3 (dependency/auth)
- Rate limit/network → exit 8 (network)
- Invalid args → exit 4

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly flags queued/stuck runs using API timestamps
- [ ] JSON output includes run IDs + workflow IDs + html_url
- [ ] Uses cached responses (short TTL)
- [ ] Works across all configured repos","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:35.923073198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983516350Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983498096Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr check command with throttle detection. Works with JSON mode.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:51:40.191796583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide

+{"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide
- [ ] Resume skips completed  {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.14","title":"Build from tag/commit for reproducibility","description":"# Build From Tag/Commit for Reproducibility

## Purpose
Ensure fallback builds are reproducible by building from the exact tag/commit that will be released.

## Behavior
- Resolve version → tag → commit SHA
- Support explicit `--ref <sha|tag>` override
- Use fresh workspace (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
- Record git SHA in manifest + logs
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations

## Git Plumbing (No Status Parsing)
- Dirty check: `git diff-index --quiet HEAD --` (exit code)
- Tag existence: `git show-ref --tags --verify refs/tags/<tag>`
- Resolve SHA: `git rev-parse <ref>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds use tagged commit, not local HEAD
- [ ] Dirty working trees produce clear errors
- [ ] Manifest contains git SHA + resolved ref

## Purpose
Choose the best host for each target based on health and enforce per-host concurrency to avoid overload.

- Select host using health + capability matrix from `hosts.yaml`

- Never exceed per-host concurrency limits
- Record queue decisions in state/logs for postmortems

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.2","title":"Setup Linux build environment on Threadripper (trj)","description":"# Setup Linux Build Environment on Threadripper (trj)

## Purpose
Ensure the local Linux host (trj) has all toolchains needed to build any tool.

## Host Details
- Alias: trj
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux (x86_64)

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Rust Nightly
```bash
# Install rustup if missing
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly toolchain
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Install cross-compilation targets
rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
rustup target add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
rustup target add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl

# Install cargo-zigbuild for easy cross-compilation
cargo install cargo-zigbuild
```

### 2. Go (Latest)
```bash
# Download and install
wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.23.linux-amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.23.linux-amd64.tar.gz
echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' >> ~/.bashrc

# Verify
go version
```

### 3. Bun (Latest)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
```

### 4. Zig (for cargo-zigbuild)
```bash
# cargo-zigbuild needs zig installed
snap install zig --classic --beta
# or
wget https://ziglang.org/download/0.13.0/zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0.tar.xz
tar xf zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0.tar.xz
sudo mv zig-linux-x86_64-0.13.0 /usr/local/zig
echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/zig' >> ~/.bashrc
```

### 5. Build Dependencies
```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y   build-essential   pkg-config   libssl-dev   mingw-w64 \           # For Windows cross-compilation
  gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu  # For ARM64 cross-compilation
```

## Verification Script
Use `scripts/toolchain_check.sh` (repo) or create a local verifier:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification ==="
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Zig: "; zig version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "MinGW: "; x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "ARM64 GCC: "; aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Done ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] cargo-zigbuild can cross-compile to Windows
- [ ] cargo-zigbuild can cross-compile to ARM64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile to all targets
- [ ] Bun can compile standalone binaries

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.3","title":"Setup macOS build environment on Mac mini (mmini)","description":"# Setup macOS Build Environment on Mac mini

## Purpose
Ensure the Mac mini (mmini) has all toolchains needed to build macOS binaries.

## Host Details
- Alias: mmini
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon ARM64)
- User: jemanuel
- Sudo: Passwordless via /etc/sudoers.d/jemanuel

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Homebrew (Package Manager)
\`\`\`bash
# Should already be installed, verify:
brew --version

# If missing:
/bin/bash -c "\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
\`\`\`

### 2. Rust Nightly
\`\`\`bash
# Install rustup
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Add x86_64 target for universal builds
rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin
\`\`\`

### 3. Go (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install go
# or download from go.dev for specific version
\`\`\`

### 4. Bun (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install oven-sh/bun/bun
# or
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
\`\`\`

### 5. Xcode Command Line Tools
\`\`\`bash
xcode-select --install
# Needed for: clang, ar, ranlib, etc.
\`\`\`

## Universal Binary Support
macOS can build both ARM64 and x86_64:

### Rust Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build for both architectures
cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin
cargo build --release --target x86_64-apple-darwin

# Create universal binary with lipo
lipo -create \\
  target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

### Go Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build ARM64
GOARCH=arm64 go build -o mytool-arm64 ./cmd/mytool

# Build x86_64
GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mytool-amd64 ./cmd/mytool

# Create universal
lipo -create mytool-arm64 mytool-amd64 -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

## Verification Script
Create ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (macOS) ==="
echo -n "Homebrew: "; brew --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Xcode CLT: "; xcode-select -p || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "lipo: "; which lipo || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Rust Targets ==="
rustup target list --installed
echo "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] Rust can build for both ARM64 and x86_64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile for both architectures
- [ ] lipo available for creating universal binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

Reuse existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` to build releases locally when GH Actions is throttled.

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3","title":"Theme 3: Release Upload Automation","description":"# Release Upload Automation

## Purpose
After local builds complete, create GitHub releases and upload binaries with proper checksums and signatures.

## The dsr release Command
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>           # Create release, upload all builds
dsr release <tool> <version> --draft   # Create as draft first
dsr release <tool> <version> --notes "..." # Custom release notes
```

## Release Workflow

### 1. Verify Builds Exist
```bash
# Check all expected artifacts exist
build_dir="$DSR_STATE_DIR/builds/$tool/$version"
for target in linux-amd64 linux-arm64 darwin-arm64 darwin-amd64 windows-amd64; do
  artifact="$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-${target}"
  [ -f "$artifact" ] || die "Missing: $artifact"
done
```

### 2. Generate Checksums (No global cd)
```bash
# Generate checksums in place
sha256sum "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-* > "$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-checksums.sha256"
```

### 3. Sign Artifacts (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
# Using minisign
for f in "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*; do
  minisign -Sm "$f" -s ~/.minisign/key.sec
done
```

### 4. Create GitHub Release
```bash
gh release create "$version" \
  --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" \
  --title "$tool $version" \
  --notes "$release_notes" \
  "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*
```

### 5. Verify Upload
```bash
gh release view "$version" --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" --json assets
```

## Release Notes Generation (Git Plumbing)
```bash
repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
last_tag=$(git -C "$repo_dir" describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^)
git -C "$repo_dir" log --oneline ${last_tag}..HEAD > "$build_dir/release_notes.txt"
```

## Asset Naming Convention
```
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
{tool}-{version}-checksums.sha256
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}.minisig
```

Examples:
```
ntm-v1.5.1-linux-amd64
ntm-v1.5.1-darwin-arm64
ntm-v1.5.1-windows-amd64.exe
ntm-v1.5.1-checksums.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates release with correct tag
- [ ] All binaries uploaded and downloadable
- [ ] Checksums file included
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated if not provided
- [ ] Works for all 16 tools

## Error Handling
- Release already exists → Prompt to update or abort
- Upload fails → Retry, then report which assets failed
- Tag doesn't exist → Create tag or error","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:45.249464757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.6","title":"Create dsr skill for Claude Code (/dsr)","description":"# Create dsr Skill for Claude Code

## Purpose
Create a skill in /cs (Claude skills directory) that teaches Claude Code how to use the dsr (Doodlestein Self-Releaser) system effectively.

## Location
/home/ubuntu/.claude/skills/dsr/SKILL.md

## Skill Should Cover

### 1. Overview
- What dsr is and why it exists
- When GH Actions throttling occurs
- The fallback build infrastructure

### 2. Commands Reference
```
dsr check <repo>              # Check if GH Actions is throttled
dsr watch                     # Monitor all repos continuously
dsr build <tool>              # Build locally
dsr release <tool> <ver>      # Create release and upload
dsr fallback <tool> <ver>     # Full pipeline (check → build → release)
dsr status                    # System + last run summary
```

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7","title":"Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts","description":"# Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts

## Purpose
Define the non-negotiable contracts that every `dsr` command adheres to: CLI flags, output streams, JSON schema, exit codes, config layering, and runtime guardrails.

## Why this matters
Reliability depends on consistency. If command behavior varies (output format, exit codes, config resolution, or path handling), automation becomes brittle and debugging becomes guesswork. This theme establishes the foundation all other themes build on.

## Scope (Must-Haves)
- **Global CLI contract** and flag behavior (incl. `--json`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run`, `--verbose`, `--quiet`)
- **Stream separation**: stderr for humans, stdout for JSON/paths only
- **Exit codes** aligned with CLI contract
- **XDG layout** for config/state/cache directories
  - `~/.config/dsr/config.yaml`
  - `~/.config/dsr/repos.d/*.yaml`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/run.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/builds/*.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/latest -> YYYY-MM-DD`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/`
- **Schema compliance** for all JSON outputs
- **Runtime guardrails** (Bash version check, path normalization, safe file ops)

## Best-Practice Parity from ru (repo_updater)
- **Bash 4+ check** with macOS Homebrew guidance when interactive
- **Path normalization** helper (accept absolute or `~`, reject relative)
- **No parsing of human output**: always use structured JSON (gh api, git plumbing)
- **Deterministic dry-run** output (stable ordering)
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** respected globally
- **Run ID + log correlation** for every command execution

## Implementation Notes
- Provide a small core helper module (e.g., `src/runtime.sh`) with:
  - `require_bash_4` (Bash version gating + instructions)
  - `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default`
  - `safe_path_under` / `safe_delete` (allowlist-based safeguards)
  - `run_cmd` (captures stdout/stderr + exit code without `set -e`)
- Main entrypoint must call `require_bash_4` early and export `DSR_NON_INTERACTIVE` / `DSR_NO_COLOR` from flags.
- Logging should write to **date-based log dirs** and update `logs/latest` safely (atomic symlink update).
- **No global cd**: all git operations use `git -C <repo>`

## Non-goals
- Implementing build/release logic (covered by other themes)
- Host-specific tooling setup (Theme 2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every command follows stream separation and exit codes
- [ ] Bash 4+ enforced with clear guidance on macOS/Linux
- [ ] Config/state/cache paths are normalized and absolute
- [ ] Safe delete/cleanup helpers prevent non-dsr path removal
- [ ] JSON schema compliance is testable for every command
- [ ] XDG state/log layout matches the spec (daily dirs + latest symlink)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:38.667058888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.1","title":"Define dsr CLI contract, exit codes, and JSON schema","description":"# Define dsr CLI Contract, Exit Codes, and JSON Schema

## Goal
Create a single, authoritative contract that every `dsr` subcommand follows. This prevents automation breakage and makes logs/JSON outputs predictable.

## Global Flags (apply to all commands)
- `--json`: machine-readable output only on stdout
- `--non-interactive` / `--yes`: disable prompts
- `--dry-run`: show planned actions without mutating state
- `--verbose` / `--quiet` / `--log-level` (debug|info|warn|error)
- `--config` / `--state-dir` / `--cache-dir`
- `--no-color`

## Runtime Rules (non-negotiable)
- Use `set -uo pipefail` (never `set -e`)
- No global `cd` for git ops; always use `git -C <path>`
- Explicit error handling: capture exit codes per step
- stderr = human output, stdout = JSON/paths only

## Exit Code Contract
- `0` success
- `1` partial failure (some targets/repos failed)
- `2` conflicts (locks, in-progress run, concurrent watch)
- `3` dependency/system error (missing gh auth, docker, etc.)
- `4` invalid arguments/config
- `5` interrupted/aborted

## JSON Output Contract (stdout only)
Top-level required fields:
- `command`, `status`, `exit_code`
- `run_id`, `started_at`, `duration_ms`
- `tool` / `version` (when applicable)
- `artifacts[]` (name, target, sha256, size_bytes)
- `warnings[]` / `errors[]` (code + message)
- `details` (command-specific payload)

## Schema Versioning
- Include `schema_version` in JSON output
- Schema changes are additive only

## Success Criteria
- [ ] CLI contract documented (README + `dsr help`)
- [ ] JSON schema defined per command
- [ ] Exit codes tested for all commands
- [ ] Examples show stderr/stdout separation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:29.014069854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:07:05.750189965Z","close_reason":"CLI contract documented in docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md, JSON schemas created in schemas/, test fixtures and validation script created","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- `dsr doctor --fix` (optional: **non-destructive** helper suggestions only)

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.4","title":"Secrets & credential loading (gh/PAT/slack)","description":"# Secrets & Credential Loading (gh/PAT/Slack)

## Purpose
Normalize how `dsr` discovers and validates credentials without leaking secrets.

## Sources (precedence)
1. Environment variables (DSR_GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK)
2. Config file entries (optional, discouraged unless encrypted)
3. `gh auth token` fallback

## Requirements
- Never echo secrets to logs
- Redact tokens in any JSON output
- Validate scopes (contents:write for releases; workflow for dispatch)
- Support non-interactive errors with actionable messages
- Never write secrets to disk unless explicitly configured

## Redaction Rules
- Any value matching token patterns must be masked in logs
- `config show --json` must redact secrets ("***")

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tokens resolved deterministically with clear precedence
- [ ] Missing/insufficient scopes detected early (doctor + runtime)
- [ ] Secrets never written to disk in plain logs
- [ ] Redaction applied consistently in structured logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:16.111579035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.109028754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:43.753410065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.903498843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.5","title":"Runtime guardrails: Bash version + path normalization","description":"# Runtime Guardrails: Bash Version + Path Normalization

## Purpose
Harden dsr runtime behavior to prevent subtle failures (Bash version mismatch, relative path writes, unsafe deletions) and mirror the reliability of `ru`.

## Requirements
- **Bash 4+ gate** with actionable guidance (macOS: brew install bash)
- **Absolute path enforcement** for `--config`, `--state-dir`, `--cache-dir` (accept `~`)
- **Safe temp dirs**: always use `/tmp` or `mktemp -d` (never create in `/data/projects`)
- **Safe delete helper**: allowlist deletions to `DSR_STATE_DIR` + `DSR_CACHE_DIR`
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** disables ANSI across all commands
- **Non-interactive** errors: never prompt; return actionable error messages

## Implementation Notes
- Add `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default` helper (ru parity)
- Add `require_bash_4` helper (exit 3 on failure)
- Add `safe_path_under <root> <path>` and `safe_rm <path>`
- Use helpers in prune/build/install flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bash 4+ required with clear macOS guidance
- [ ] Relative paths rejected (with helpful error)
- [ ] Safe delete helper prevents accidental removal outside dsr dirs
